data.text:
## Claim:
claim: "TabM — a parameter-efficient ensemble of MLPs using BatchEnsemble-style shared weights with per-submodel adapters — outperforms simple MLP baselines and prior tabular DL architectures on tabular supervised learning benchmarks. Core claim under test: the ensembling machinery (k=32 submodels with adapter-induced diversity) produces materially better predictions than a plain MLP via averaging the k submodel outputs."

1	# License: https://github.com/yandex-research/tabm/blob/main/LICENSE
2	"""TabM: Advancing Tabular Deep Learning with Parameter-Efficient Ensembling (ICLR 2025)."""  # noqa: E501
3	
4	__version__ = '0.0.3'
5	
6	import abc
7	import collections.abc
8	import typing
9	import warnings
10	from dataclasses import dataclass
11	from typing import Any, Literal, Optional, TypedDict, Union
12	
13	import rtdl_num_embeddings
14	import torch
15	import torch.nn as nn
16	from torch import Tensor
17	from torch.nn.parameter import Parameter
18	from typing_extensions import Unpack
19	
20	# ======================================================================================
21	# Utilities
22	# ======================================================================================
23	_INTERNAL_ERROR_MESSAGE = (
24	    'Internal error (this code must be unreachable; please, report a bug)'
25	)
26	
27	
28	def _get_required_kwarg(kwargs, key: str) -> Any:
29	    if key not in kwargs:
30	        raise TypeError(f'The required argument `{key}` is missing')
31	    return kwargs[key]
32	
33	
34	def _check_kwargs(
35	    kwargs,
36	    *,
37	    forbidden: Optional[list[str]] = None,
38	) -> None:
39	    assert isinstance(kwargs, dict), _INTERNAL_ERROR_MESSAGE
40	    if forbidden is not None:
41	        for argname in forbidden:
42	            if argname in kwargs:
43	                raise TypeError(f'The argument {argname} must not be passed explicitly')
44	
45	
46	def _check_positive_integer(value: int, name: str) -> None:
47	    if value <= 0:
48	        raise ValueError(f'{name} must be a positive integer, however: {name}={value}')
49	
50	
51	def _check_input_ndim(value: Tensor, name: str, required_ndim: int) -> None:
52	    if value.ndim != required_ndim:
53	        raise ValueError(
54	            f'The input must have {required_ndim} dimensions, however:'
55	            f' {name}.ndim={value.ndim}'
56	        )
57	
58	
59	def _check_input_min_ndim(value: Tensor, name: str, required_min_ndim: int) -> None:
60	    if value.ndim < required_min_ndim:
61	        raise ValueError(
62	            f'The input must have at least {required_min_ndim} dimensions,'
63	            f' however: {name}.ndim={value.ndim}'
64	        )
65	
66	
67	def _check_ensemble_input_shape(name: str, value: Tensor, k: int) -> None:
68	    _check_input_ndim(value, name, 3)
69	    if value.shape[-2] != k:
70	        raise ValueError(
71	            f'The penultimate input dimension must be equal to the ensemble size {k=},'
72	            f' however: {name}.shape[-2]={value.shape[-2]}'
73	        )
74	
75	
76	# ======================================================================================
77	# Initialization
78	# ======================================================================================
79	def _init_rsqrt_uniform_(tensor: Tensor, d: int) -> Tensor:
80	    assert d > 0, _INTERNAL_ERROR_MESSAGE
81	    d_rsqrt = d**-0.5
82	    return nn.init.uniform_(tensor, -d_rsqrt, d_rsqrt)
83	
84	
85	@torch.inference_mode()
86	def _init_random_signs_(tensor: Tensor) -> Tensor:
87	    return tensor.bernoulli_(0.5).mul_(2).add_(-1)
88	
89	
90	ScalingRandomInitialization = Literal['random-signs', 'normal']
91	ScalingInitialization = Literal[ScalingRandomInitialization, 'ones']
92	
93	
94	def init_scaling_(
95	    x: Tensor,
96	    distribution: ScalingInitialization,
97	    chunks: Optional[list[int]] = None,
98	) -> Tensor:
99	    """Initialize scaling parameters.
100	
101	    Args:
102	        x: the tensor storing the scaling parameters.
103	        distribution: the initialization distribution.
104	        chunks: the initialization chunks.
105	    """
106	    if distribution == 'ones':
107	        if chunks is not None:
108	            raise ValueError(f'When {distribution=}, chunks must be None')
109	        init_fn = nn.init.ones_
110	    elif distribution == 'normal':
111	        init_fn = nn.init.normal_
112	    elif distribution == 'random-signs':
113	        init_fn = _init_random_signs_
114	    else:
115	        raise ValueError(f'Unknown {distribution=}')
116	
117	    if chunks is None:
118	        return init_fn(x)
119	
120	    else:
121	        if x.ndim < 1:
122	            raise ValueError(
123	                'When chunks is not None, the input tensor must have at least one'
124	                f'dimension, however: {x.ndim=}'
125	            )
126	        if sum(chunks) != x.shape[-1]:
127	            raise ValueError(
128	                'The tensor shape and chunks are incompatible:'
129	                f' {x.shape[-1]=} != {sum(chunks)=}'
130	            )
131	
132	        # More generally, for a given set of leading dimensions,
133	        # all values within one chunk are initialized with the same (random) value.
134	        #
135	        # Consider an example:
136	        # - x.shape      == (4, 5)
137	        # - distribution == 'normal'
138	        # - chunks       == [2, 3]
139	        #
140	        # Then, each of the four rows of x is split in two chunks of sizes 2 and 3.
141	        # And each chunk is initialized with the same value sampled randomly from
142	        # the normal distribution. As a result, x is initialized as follows:
143	        #
144	        # [
145	        #     [a1 a1 b1 b1 b1]
146	        #     [a2 a2 b2 b2 b2]
147	        #     [a3 a3 b3 b3 b3]
148	        #     [a4 a4 b4 b4 b4]
149	        # ]
150	        #
151	        # where ai and bi are the randomly sampled values (i denotes the row index,
152	        # and a/b refers to the first/second chunk of a given row).
153	        with torch.inference_mode():
154	            chunk_start = 0
155	            for chunk_size in chunks:
156	                x[..., chunk_start : chunk_start + chunk_size] = init_fn(
157	                    torch.empty(*x.shape[:-1], 1)
158	                )
159	                chunk_start += chunk_size
160	        return x
161	
162	
163	# ======================================================================================
164	# Basics modules
165	# ======================================================================================
166	class _OneHotEncoding(nn.Module):
167	    """One-hot encoding for categorical features.
168	
169	    The output of the module is the concatenation of one-hot representations of the
170	    categorical features.
171	
172	    **Shape**
173	
174	    - Input: ``(*, len(cardinalities))``,
175	          where `*` denotes an arbitrary number of batch dimensions.
176	    - Output: ``(*, sum(cardinalities))``.
177	
178	    **Examples**
179	
180	    >>> cardinalities = [2, 3]
181	    >>> m = _OneHotEncoding(cardinalities)
182	    >>> x = torch.tensor([
183	    ...     [0, 0],
184	    ...     [1, 2],
185	    ... ])
186	    >>> m(x)
187	    tensor([[1, 0, 1, 0, 0],
188	            [0, 1, 0, 0, 1]])
189	    """
190	
191	    def __init__(self, cardinalities: list[int]) -> None:
192	        """
193	        Args:
194	            cardinalities: the cardinalities of the categorical features,
195	                i.e. ``cardinalities[i]`` must store the number of unique categories
196	                of the i-th categorical feature.
197	        """
198	        if not cardinalities:
199	            raise ValueError(
200	                f'cardinalities must be non-empty, however: {cardinalities=}'
201	            )
202	        for i, cardinality in enumerate(cardinalities):
203	            if cardinality <= 0:
204	                raise ValueError(
205	                    'cardinalities must be a list of positive'
206	                    f' integers, however: cardinalities[{i}]={cardinalities[i]}'
207	                )
208	
209	        super().__init__()
210	        self._cardinalities = cardinalities
211	
212	    def get_output_shape(self) -> torch.Size:
213	        """Get the output shape without the batch dimensions."""
214	        return torch.Size((sum(self._cardinalities),))
215	
216	    def forward(self, x: Tensor) -> Tensor:
217	        """Do the forward pass.
218	
219	        Args:
220	            x: the categorical features. The data type must be `torch.long`.
221	               The i-th feature must take values in ``range(0, cardinalities[i])``,
222	               where ``cardinalities`` is the list passed to the constructor.
223	        """
224	        _check_input_min_ndim(x, 'x', 1)
225	        if x.shape[-1] != len(self._cardinalities):
226	            raise ValueError(
227	                'Based on the cardinalities passed to the constructor, the input must'
228	                f' have the shape (*, {len(self._cardinalities)}), however: {x.shape=}'
229	            )
230	
231	        return torch.cat(
232	            [
233	                nn.functional.one_hot(x[..., i], cardinality)
234	                for i, cardinality in enumerate(self._cardinalities)
235	            ],
236	            -1,
237	        )
238	
239	
240	class ElementwiseAffine(nn.Module):
241	    """Elementwise affine transformation.
242	
243	    **Shape**
244	
245	    - Input: ``(*, *shape)``,
246	          where ``*`` denotes an arbitrary number of batch dimensions.
247	    - Output: ``(*, *shape)``
248	
249	    **Examples**
250	
251	    >>> m = ElementwiseAffine(
252	    ...     (3, 4),
253	    ...     scaling_init='normal',
254	    ...     scaling_init_chunks=None,
255	    ...     bias=True,
256	    ... )
257	    >>> x = torch.randn((1, 2, 3, 4))
258	    >>> m(x).shape
259	    torch.Size([1, 2, 3, 4])
260	    """
261	
262	    bias: Optional[Tensor]
263	
264	    def __init__(
265	        self,
266	        shape: tuple[int, ...],
267	        *,
268	        bias: bool,
269	        scaling_init: ScalingInitialization,
270	        scaling_init_chunks: Optional[list[int]] = None,
271	        dtype: Optional[torch.dtype] = None,
272	        device: Optional[Union[str, torch.dtype]] = None,
273	    ) -> None:
274	        """
275	        Args:
276	            shape: the input shape without the batch dimensions.
277	            scaling_init: the initialization of the scaling.
278	            scaling_init_chunks: the initialization chunks of the scaling
279	                (see README of the package for details).
280	            bias: if True, the module will have a trainable bias.
281	            dtype: the parameter data type.
282	            device: the parameter device.
283	        """
284	        super().__init__()
285	        factory_kwargs = {'device': device, 'dtype': dtype}
286	        self.weight = Parameter(torch.empty(shape, **factory_kwargs))  # type: ignore[call-overload]
287	        self.register_parameter(
288	            'bias',
289	            Parameter(torch.empty(shape, **factory_kwargs)) if bias else None,  # type: ignore[call-overload]
290	        )
291	        self._weight_init = scaling_init
292	        self._weight_init_chunks = scaling_init_chunks
293	        self.reset_parameters()
294	
295	    def reset_parameters(self) -> None:
296	        init_scaling_(self.weight, self._weight_init, self._weight_init_chunks)
297	        if self.bias is not None:
298	            nn.init.zeros_(self.bias)
299	
300	    def forward(self, x: Tensor) -> Tensor:
301	        """Do the forward pass."""
302	        expected_shape = self.weight.shape
303	        expected_shape_ndim = len(expected_shape)
304	        _check_input_min_ndim(x, 'x', expected_shape_ndim)
305	        if x.shape[-expected_shape_ndim:] != expected_shape:
306	            raise ValueError(
307	                'The input must have the shape'
308	                f' (*, {", ".join(map(str, expected_shape))}), however: {x.shape=}'
309	            )
310	
311	        return (
312	            x * self.weight
313	            if self.bias is None
314	            else torch.addcmul(self.bias, self.weight, x)
315	        )
316	
317	
318	# ======================================================================================
319	# Ensemble modules
320	# ======================================================================================
321	def ensemble_view(x: Tensor, k: int, training: bool) -> Tensor:
322	    """The functional form of `EnsembleView`.
323	
324	    Args:
325	        x: the tensor.
326	        k: the ensemble size.
327	        training: the training status.
328	    """
329	    if x.ndim == 2:
330	        x = x.unsqueeze(-2).expand(-1, k, -1)
331	
332	    elif x.ndim == 3:
333	        if x.shape[-2] != k:
334	            raise ValueError(
335	                f'The penultimate input dimension must be equal to k={k},'
336	                f' however: {x.shape[-2]=}'
337	            )
338	        if not training:
339	            warnings.warn(
340	                'When training=False, the input should usually have the shape'
341	                ' (batch_size, d), i.e. hold one representation per object.'
342	                f' However: {x.shape=}. Is this intentional?'
343	            )
344	
345	    else:
346	        raise ValueError(
347	            f'The must must have either two or three dimensions, however: {x.ndim=}'
348	        )
349	
350	    return x
351	
352	
353	class EnsembleView(nn.Module):
354	    """Turn a tensor to a valid ensemble input.
355	
356	    More precisely:
357	
358	    * A two-dimensional tensors of the shape ``(batch_size, d)`` are expanded to
359	      three-dimensional tensors of the shape ``(batch_size, k, d)`` holding
360	      ``k`` identical views of the original input.
361	    * Three-dimensional tensors are propagated as-is without any changes.
362	      In this case, the second input dimension must be equal to the ensemble size ``k``.
363	
364	    `EnsembleView` is a starting module for ensembles of MLP-like models (i.e. where the
365	    *base* model takes a single tensor of the shape ``(batch_tensor, d)`` as input).
366	    Usually, `EnsembleView` should be placed right before the first ensemble module.
367	
368	    .. note::
369	        The module uses `torch.expand` under the hood and thus does not create any
370	        copies.
371	
372	    **Shape**
373	
374	    - Input: ``(batch_size, d)`` or ``(batch_size, k, d)``
375	    - Output: ``(batch_size, k, d)``
376	
377	    **Examples**
378	
379	    Two-dimensional tensors are expanded to three-dimensional tensors:
380	
381	    >>> m = EnsembleView(k=2)
382	    >>> x = torch.randn(3, 4)
383	    >>> m(x).shape
384	    torch.Size([3, 2, 4])
385	    >>> torch.all(m(x) == x.unsqueeze(-2)).item()
386	    True
387	
388	    Three-dimensional tensors are returned as-is:
389	
390	    >>> m = EnsembleView(k=2)
391	    >>> x = torch.randn(3, 2, 4)
392	    >>> m(x) is x
393	    True
394	    """
395	
396	    def __init__(self, *, k: int) -> None:
397	        """
398	        Args:
399	            k: the ensemble size.
400	        """
401	        super().__init__()
402	        self._k = k
403	
404	    @property
405	    def k(self) -> int:
406	        """The ensemble size."""
407	        return self._k
408	
409	    def forward(self, x: Tensor) -> Tensor:
410	        """Do the forward pass."""
411	        return ensemble_view(x, self.k, self.training)
412	
413	
414	class LinearEnsemble(nn.Module):
415	    """An ensemble of k linear layers applied in parallel to k inputs.
416	
417	    The i-th linear layer is applied to the i-th input matrix of the shape
418	    ``(batch_size, in_features)`` and produces the i-th output matrix of the shape
419	    ``(batch_size, out_features)``.
420	
421	    **Shape**
422	
423	    - Input: ``(batch_size, k, in_features)``
424	    - Output: ``(batch_size, k, out_features)``
425	
426	    **Examples**
427	
428	    >>> m = LinearEnsemble(2, 3, k=4)
429	    >>> x = torch.randn(5, 4, 2)
430	    >>> m(x).shape
431	    torch.Size([5, 4, 3])
432	    """
433	
434	    bias: Optional[Tensor]
435	
436	    def __init__(
437	        self,
438	        in_features: int,
439	        out_features: int,
440	        bias: bool = True,
441	        *,
442	        k: int,
443	        dtype: Optional[torch.dtype] = None,
444	        device: Optional[Union[str, torch.dtype]] = None,
445	    ) -> None:
446	        """
447	        Args:
448	            in_features: the input size of each layer.
449	            out_features: the output size of each layer.
450	            bias: determines if the layers have biases.
451	            k: the number of linear layers.
452	            dtype: the parameter data type.
453	            device: the parameter device.
454	        """
455	        super().__init__()
456	        factory_kwargs = {'device': device, 'dtype': dtype}
457	        self.weight = Parameter(
458	            torch.empty(k, in_features, out_features, **factory_kwargs)  # type: ignore[call-overload]
459	        )
460	        self.register_parameter(
461	            'bias',
462	            Parameter(torch.empty(k, out_features, **factory_kwargs)) if bias else None,  # type: ignore[call-overload]
463	        )
464	        self.reset_parameters()
465	
466	    @classmethod
467	    def from_linear(cls, module: nn.Linear, **kwargs) -> 'LinearEnsemble':
468	        """Create an instance from its non-ensemble version.
469	
470	        Args:
471	            module: the original module.
472	            kwargs: the ensemble-specific arguments.
473	        """
474	        kwargs.setdefault('dtype', module.weight.dtype)
475	        kwargs.setdefault('device', module.weight.device)
476	        return cls(
477	            module.in_features, module.out_features, module.bias is not None, **kwargs
478	        )
479	
480	    @property
481	    def in_features(self) -> int:
482	        """The input dimension."""
483	        return self.weight.shape[-2]
484	
485	    @property
486	    def out_features(self) -> int:
487	        """The output dimension."""
488	        return self.weight.shape[-1]
489	
490	    @property
491	    def k(self) -> int:
492	        """The ensemble size."""
493	        return self.weight.shape[0]
494	
495	    def reset_parameters(self):
496	        d = self.in_features
497	        _init_rsqrt_uniform_(self.weight, d)
498	        if self.bias is not None:
499	            _init_rsqrt_uniform_(self.bias, d)
500	
501	    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
502	        """Do the forward pass."""
503	        _check_ensemble_input_shape('x', x, self.k)
504	        if x.shape[-1] != self.in_features:
505	            raise ValueError(
506	                f'The last input dimension must be equal to {self.in_features},'
507	                f' however: {x.shape[-1]}'
508	            )
509	
510	        x = x.transpose(0, 1)
511	        x = x @ self.weight
512	        x = x.transpose(0, 1)
513	        if self.bias is not None:
514	            x = x + self.bias
515	        return x
516	
517	
518	class LinearBatchEnsemble(nn.Module):
519	    """A parameter-efficient ensemble of $k$ linear layers applied in parallel to $k$ inputs.
520	
521	    The implementation follows the paper
522	    "BatchEnsemble: An Alternative Approach to Efficient Ensemble and Lifelong Learning".
523	    The only addition is the arguments related to the initialization of the $R$ and $S$ matrices,
524	    which is used by TabM.
525	
526	    .. note::
527	        The notation related to the $R$ and $S$ matrices may be not fully in sync
528	        between the TabM paper and this code.
529	
530	    .. note::
531	        In the TabM paper, the matrices $R$ and $S$, as well as the bias, are called
532	        "adapters". This term was introduced in the paper only to tell the story, and was *not*
533	        used in the original BatchEnsemble paper. So the implementation of this class
534	        also avoids the term "adapter".
535	
536	    **Shape**
537	
538	    - Input: ``(batch_size, k, in_features)``
539	    - Output: ``(batch_size, k, out_features)``
540	
541	    **Examples**
542	
543	    >>> m = LinearBatchEnsemble(4, 5, k=3, scaling_init='random-signs')
544	    >>> x = torch.randn(2, 3, 4)
545	    >>> m(x).shape
546	    torch.Size([2, 3, 5])
547	    """  # noqa: E501
548	
549	    bias: Optional[Tensor]
550	
551	    def __init__(
552	        self,
553	        in_features: int,
554	        out_features: int,
555	        bias: bool = True,
556	        *,
557	        k: int,
558	        scaling_init: Union[
559	            ScalingInitialization, tuple[ScalingInitialization, ScalingInitialization]
560	        ],
561	        first_scaling_init_chunks: Optional[list[int]] = None,
562	        dtype: Optional[torch.dtype] = None,
563	        device: Optional[Union[str, torch.dtype]] = None,
564	    ):
565	        """
566	        Args:
567	            in_features: the input size of each layer.
568	            out_features: the output size of each layer.
569	            bias: determines if the layers have biases.
570	            k: the number of linear layers.
571	            scaling_init: the initialization distribution for the scaling parameters.
572	                A tuple can be passed to set different distributions for the first
573	                and second scaling parameters.
574	            first_scaling_init_chunks: the initialization chunks of the first scaling
575	                (see README of the package for details).
576	            dtype: the parameter data type.
577	            device: the parameter device.
578	        """
579	        _check_positive_integer(in_features, 'in_features')
580	        _check_positive_integer(out_features, 'out_features')
581	        _check_positive_integer(k, 'k')
582	
583	        super().__init__()
584	        factory_kwargs = {'device': device, 'dtype': dtype}
585	        self.weight = Parameter(
586	            torch.empty(out_features, in_features, **factory_kwargs)  # type: ignore[call-overload]
587	        )
588	        self.r = Parameter(  # R
589	            torch.empty(k, in_features, **factory_kwargs)  # type: ignore[call-overload]
590	        )
591	        self.s = Parameter(  # S
592	            torch.empty(k, out_features, **factory_kwargs)  # type: ignore[call-overload]
593	        )
594	        self.register_parameter(
595	            'bias',
596	            Parameter(torch.empty(k, out_features, **factory_kwargs)) if bias else None,  # type: ignore[call-overload]
597	        )
598	
599	        self.in_features = in_features
600	        self.out_features = out_features
601	        self.k = k
602	        if isinstance(scaling_init, tuple):
603	            self._first_scaling_init = scaling_init[0]
604	            self._second_scaling_init = scaling_init[1]
605	        else:
606	            self._first_scaling_init = scaling_init
607	            self._second_scaling_init = scaling_init

612	    @classmethod
613	    def from_linear(cls, module: nn.Linear, **kwargs) -> 'LinearBatchEnsemble':
614	        """Create an instance from its non-ensemble version.
615	
616	        Args:
617	            module: the original module.
618	            kwargs: the ensemble-specific arguments.
619	        """
620	        kwargs.setdefault('dtype', module.weight.dtype)
621	        kwargs.setdefault('device', module.weight.device)
622	        return cls(
623	            module.in_features, module.out_features, module.bias is not None, **kwargs
624	        )

626	    def reset_parameters(self) -> None:
627	        _init_rsqrt_uniform_(self.weight, self.in_features)
628	        init_scaling_(self.r, self._first_scaling_init, self._first_scaling_init_chunks)
629	        init_scaling_(self.s, self._second_scaling_init, None)
630	        if self.bias is not None:
631	            bias_init = torch.empty(
632	                # NOTE
633	                # All k biases have the same initialization (this is why the shape of
634	                # bias_init is (out_features,) instead of (k, out_features)).
635	                # This is similar to having one shared bias plus
636	                # k zero-initialized non-shared biases.
637	                self.out_features,
638	                dtype=self.weight.dtype,
639	                device=self.weight.device,
640	            )
641	            bias_init = _init_rsqrt_uniform_(bias_init, self.in_features)
642	            with torch.inference_mode():
643	                self.bias.copy_(bias_init)

645	    def forward(self, x: Tensor) -> Tensor:
646	        """Do the forward pass."""
647	        _check_input_ndim(x, 'x', 3)  # (B, K, D)

649	        # >>> Equation (5) from the BatchEnsemble paper (arXiv v2).
650	        x = x * self.r
651	        x = x @ self.weight.T
652	        x = x * self.s
653	        # <<<
654	
655	        if self.bias is not None:
656	            x = x + self.bias
657	        return x

660	class BatchNorm1dEnsemble(nn.Module):
661	    """An ensemble of $k$ batch normalizations applied in parallel to $k$ inputs.
662	
663	    The module can be used in ensembles of MLP-like models.
664	    Similarly to other ensemble modules in this package, the $k$ normalizations are
665	    fully independent, i.e. they do not share the running statistics nor the
666	    parameters of the affine transformations.
667	
668	    **Shape**
669	
670	    - Input: ``(batch_size, k, num_features)``
671	    - Output: ``(batch_size, k, num_features)``
672	
673	    **Examples**
674	
675	    >>> m = BatchNorm1dEnsemble(2, k=3)
676	    >>> x = torch.randn(4, 3, 2)
677	    >>> m(x).shape
678	    torch.Size([4, 3, 2])
679	    """
680	
681	    def __init__(
682	        self, num_features: int, *, k: int, affine: bool = True, **kwargs
683	    ) -> None:
684	        """
685	        Args:
686	            num_features: same as in `BatchNorm1d`.
687	            affine: same as in `BatchNorm1d`.
688	            k: the ensemble size.
689	            kwargs: the (optional) keyword arguments for `BatchNorm1d`.
690	        """
691	        _check_positive_integer(k, 'k')
692	
693	        super().__init__()
694	        self.impl = nn.BatchNorm1d(k * num_features, affine=False, **kwargs)
695	        self.affine = (
696	            ElementwiseAffine(
697	                (k, num_features),
698	                scaling_init='ones',
699	                scaling_init_chunks=None,
700	                bias=True,
701	                dtype=kwargs.get('dtype'),
702	                device=kwargs.get('device'),
703	            )
704	            if affine
705	            else None
706	        )
707	        self._k = k
708	
709	    @classmethod
710	    def from_batchnorm1d(
711	        cls, module: nn.BatchNorm1d, **kwargs
712	    ) -> 'BatchNorm1dEnsemble':
713	        """Create an instance from its non-ensemble version.
714	
715	        Args:
716	            module: the original module.
717	            kwargs: the ensemble-specific arguments.
718	        """
719	        kwargs.update(
720	            (key, getattr(module, key)) for key in nn.BatchNorm1d.__constants__
721	        )
722	        if module.weight is not None:
723	            kwargs['dtype'] = module.weight.dtype
724	            kwargs['device'] = module.weight.device
725	        elif module.running_mean is not None:
726	            kwargs['dtype'] = module.running_mean.dtype
727	            kwargs['device'] = module.running_mean.device
728	        return BatchNorm1dEnsemble(**kwargs)
729	
730	    @property
731	    def k(self) -> int:
732	        """The ensemble size."""
733	        return self._k
734	
735	    def forward(self, x: Tensor) -> Tensor:
736	        """Do the forward pass."""
737	        _check_ensemble_input_shape('x', x, self.k)
738	
739	        x = self.impl(x.flatten(-2)).unflatten(-1, x.shape[-2:])

741	            x = self.affine(x)

748	    * Contrary to other ensemble modules in this package, this modules does not impose
749	      any restriction on the input shape of the base normalization.
750	    * Similarly to other ensemble modules in this package, the $k$ normalizations are
751	      fully independent, i.e. they do not share the running statistics nor the
752	      weights of the affine transformations.
753	
754	    **Shape**
755	
756	    - Input: ``(*, k, *normalized_shape)``, where ``*`` denotes an arbitrary number of
757	          batch dimensions
758	    - Output: ``(*, k, *normalized_shape)``
759	
760	    **Examples**
761	
762	    >>> m = LayerNormEnsemble(2, k=3)
763	    >>> x = torch.randn(4, 3, 2)
764	    >>> m(x).shape
765	    torch.Size([4, 3, 2])
766	
767	    >>> m = LayerNormEnsemble((2, 3), k=4)
768	    >>> x = torch.randn(5, 6, 4, 2, 3)
769	    >>> m(x).shape
770	    torch.Size([5, 6, 4, 2, 3])
771	    """
772	
773	    def __init__(
774	        self,
775	        normalized_shape: Union[int, list[int], torch.Size],
776	        *,
777	        k: int,
778	        elementwise_affine: bool = True,
779	        **kwargs,
780	    ) -> None:
781	        """
782	        Args:
783	            normalized_shape: same as in `LayerNorm`.
784	            k: the ensemble size.
785	            elementwise_affine: same as in `LayerNorm`.
786	            kwargs: the (optional) keyword arguments for `LayerNorm`.
787	        """

790	        super().__init__()
791	        self.impl = nn.LayerNorm(normalized_shape, elementwise_affine=False, **kwargs)
792	        self.affine = (
793	            ElementwiseAffine(
794	                (k, *self.impl.normalized_shape),
795	                scaling_init='ones',
796	                scaling_init_chunks=None,
797	                bias=True,
798	                dtype=kwargs.get('dtype'),
799	                device=kwargs.get('device'),
800	            )
801	            if elementwise_affine
802	            else None
803	        )
804	        self._k = k

806	    @classmethod
807	    def from_layernorm(cls, module: nn.LayerNorm, **kwargs) -> 'LayerNormEnsemble':
808	        """Create an instance from its non-ensemble version.
809	
810	        Args:
811	            module: the original module.
812	            kwargs: the ensemble-specific arguments.
813	        """
814	        kwargs.update((key, getattr(module, key)) for key in nn.LayerNorm.__constants__)
815	        if module.weight is not None:
816	            kwargs['dtype'] = module.weight.dtype
817	            kwargs['device'] = module.weight.device
818	        elif module.running_mean is not None:
819	            kwargs['dtype'] = module.running_mean.dtype
820	            kwargs['device'] = module.running_mean.device

823	    @property
824	    def k(self) -> int:
825	        """The ensemble size."""
826	        return self._k

828	    def forward(self, x: Tensor) -> Tensor:
829	        """Do the forward pass."""
830	        expected_shape = (self.k, *self.impl.normalized_shape)
831	        expected_shape_ndim = len(expected_shape)
832	        _check_input_min_ndim(x, 'x', expected_shape_ndim)
833	        if x.shape[-expected_shape_ndim:] != expected_shape:
834	            raise ValueError(
835	                'The input must have the shape'
836	                f' (*, {", ".join(map(str, expected_shape))}), however: {x.shape=}'
837	            )

839	        x = self.impl(x)
840	        if self.affine is not None:
841	            x = self.affine(x)

848	def _check_include_exclude(
849	    module: nn.Module,
850	    *,
851	    Layer: type[nn.Module],
852	    include: Optional[list[nn.Module]],
853	    exclude: Optional[list[nn.Module]],
854	    **kwargs,
855	) -> None:
856	    del kwargs
857	
858	    if include is not None and exclude is not None:
859	        raise ValueError('include and exclude cannot be provided simultaneously')
860	    if include is not None:
861	        if any(not isinstance(x, Layer) for x in include):
862	            raise ValueError(f'All modules in `include` must be instances of {Layer=}')
863	    if exclude is not None:
864	        if any(not isinstance(x, Layer) for x in exclude):
865	            raise ValueError(f'All modules in `exclude` must be instances of {Layer=}')
866	
867	    for list_name, list_values in [('include', include), ('exclude', exclude)]:
868	        if list_values is not None:
869	            submodules = list(module.modules())
870	            if any(x not in submodules for x in list_values):
871	                raise ValueError(
872	                    f'Some of the modules in `{list_name}` are not submodules'
873	                    ' of the provided module'
874	                )

877	def _replace_layers_impl_(
878	    module: nn.Module,
879	    *,
880	    Layer: type[nn.Module],
881	    factory: collections.abc.Callable[..., nn.Module],
882	    include: Optional[list[nn.Module]],
883	    exclude: Optional[list[nn.Module]],
884	    **kwargs,
885	) -> None:
886	    for name, submodule in list(module.named_children()):
887	        if isinstance(submodule, Layer):
888	            if (
889	                (exclude is None or all(submodule is not x for x in exclude))
890	                if include is None
891	                else any(submodule is x for x in include)
892	            ):
893	                module.add_module(name, factory(submodule, **kwargs))
894	        else:
895	            _replace_layers_impl_(
896	                module=submodule,
897	                Layer=Layer,
898	                factory=factory,
899	                **kwargs,
900	                include=include,
901	                exclude=exclude,
902	            )
903	
904	
905	def _replace_layers_(**kwargs) -> None:
906	    _check_include_exclude(**kwargs)
907	    return _replace_layers_impl_(**kwargs)

1302	    as a minimum possible architectural approach to ensembling, hence the name.
1303	    """

1306	        self,
1307	        *,
1308	        affine_bias: bool,  # TabM uses False
1309	        affine_scaling_init: ScalingRandomInitialization,
1310	        affine_scaling_init_chunks: Optional[list[int]] = None,
1311	        **kwargs: Unpack[MLPBackboneEnsembleKwargs],
1312	    ) -> None:
1313	        """
1314	        Args:

1316	            affine_scaling_init_chunks: the initialization chunks of the scaling
1317	                (see README of the package for details).

1323	            (self.k, self.blocks[0][0].weight.shape[-1]),

1326	            bias=affine_bias,

1333	        self,
1334	        index: int,

1337	        options: Any,

1525	class TabM(nn.Module):
1526	    """TabM -- a Tabular DL model that makes Multiple predictions.
1527	
1528	    Technically, one TabM efficiently represents an ensemble of $k$ MLPs.
1529	    The two key differences of TabM compared to a conventional deep ensemble of MLPs:
1530	
1531	    * **Parallel training** of all $k$ MLPs. This allows monitoring the performance
1532	      of the ensemle during training and stopping the training when it is optimal
1533	      for the ensemble, not for individual MLPs.
1534	    * **Weight sharing** between the MLPs. Not only this significantly improves the
1535	      runtime and memory efficiency, but also surves as an effective regularization
1536	      that further improves the performance.
1537	
1538	    **Shape**
1539	
1540	    (A) When passing the same batch to each of the $k$ submodels:
1541	
1542	    - Input:
1543	        - ``x_num``: ``(batch_size, n_num_features)``
1544	        - ``x_cat``: ``(batch_size, len(cat_cardinalities))``
1545	    - Output: ``(batch_size, k, d_block if d_out is None else d_out)``
1546	
1547	    (B) When passing different batches to the $k$ submodels:
1548	
1549	    .. note::
1550	
1551	        One use case for this strategy is training the $k$ underlying MLPs on
1552	        different batches. In this case, the training input should consist of $k$
1553	        full-fledged batches, **NOT** of one batch reshaped to $k$ small batches.
1554	        The official notebook provides an example of how to implement this.
1555	
1556	    - Input:
1557	        - ``x_num``: ``(batch_size, k, n_num_features)``
1558	        - ``x_cat``: ``(batch_size, k, len(cat_cardinalities))``
1559	    - Output: ``(batch_size, k, d_block if d_out is None else d_out)`` (no change)
1560	    """
1561	
1562	    num_module: Optional[nn.Module]
1563	    cat_module: Optional[nn.Module]
1564	
1565	    def __init__(
1566	        self,
1567	        *,
1568	        n_num_features: int = 0,
1569	        cat_cardinalities: Optional[list[int]] = None,
1570	        d_out: Optional[int],
1571	        num_embeddings: Optional[_NumEmbeddings] = None,
1572	        **backbone_kwargs,
1573	    ) -> None:
1574	        """
1575	        .. note::
1576	            Consider using `TabM.make` instead of this method.
1577	
1578	        Args:
1579	            n_num_features: the number of numerical (continuous) features.
1580	            cat_cardinalities: the cardinalities of the categorical features,
1581	                i.e. ``cardinalities[i]`` must store the number of unique categories
1582	                of the i-th categorical feature. The categorical features are encoded
1583	                with the one-hot encoding.
1584	            d_out: the output size. If None, the module's output will be
1585	                the output of the $k$ underlying backbones.
1586	            num_embeddings: the embeddings for numerical features that transform the
1587	                ``x_num`` input before applying the MLP backbones.
1588	                This module is fully shared between the $k$ MLPs.
1589	            backbone_kwargs: all arguments of `make_tabm_backbone`, except for
1590	                ``d_in`` and ``start_scaling_init_chunks`` (they will be inferred
1591	                automatically).
1592	        """
1593	        _check_kwargs(backbone_kwargs, forbidden=['d_in', 'start_scaling_init_chunks'])
1594	
1595	        if cat_cardinalities is None:
1596	            cat_cardinalities = []
1597	
1598	        if n_num_features < 0:
1599	            raise ValueError(
1600	                f'n_num_features must be a non-negative integer, however:'
1601	                f' {n_num_features=}'
1602	            )
1603	        if n_num_features == 0 and not cat_cardinalities:
1604	            raise ValueError(
1605	                f'{n_num_features=} and {cat_cardinalities=} at the same time,'
1606	                ' which is not allowed'
1607	            )
1608	        if n_num_features == 0 and num_embeddings is not None:
1609	            raise ValueError(f'When {n_num_features=}, num_embeddings must be None')
1610	        if num_embeddings is not None:
1611	            # Unknown embedding types can result in all kinds of weird things,
1612	            # so checking the type explicitly.
1613	            if not isinstance(num_embeddings, typing.get_args(_NumEmbeddings)):
1614	                raise TypeError(
1615	                    f'num_embeddings of the type {type(num_embeddings)} is not'
1616	                    ' supported'
1617	                )
1618	            if (
1619	                isinstance(
1620	                    num_embeddings, rtdl_num_embeddings.PiecewiseLinearEmbeddings
1621	                )
1622	                and num_embeddings.linear0 is None
1623	            ):
1624	                raise ValueError(
1625	                    'When using PiecewiseLinearEmbeddings as num_embeddings,'
1626	                    ' set the version argument to "B":'
1627	                    '\nnum_embeddings = PiecewiseLinearEmbeddings(..., version="B")'
1628	                )

1630	        super().__init__()
1631	
1632	        d_features: list[int] = []  # Representation sizes of all features.
1633	
1634	        if num_embeddings is None:
1635	            d_features.extend(1 for _ in range(n_num_features))
1636	        else:
1637	            num_embeddings_n_features, d_num_embedding = (
1638	                num_embeddings.get_output_shape()
1639	            )
1640	            if num_embeddings_n_features != n_num_features:
1641	                raise ValueError(
1642	                    'num_embeddings was created for a different number of features'
1643	                    f' than {n_num_features=}'
1644	                )
1645	            d_features.extend(d_num_embedding for _ in range(n_num_features))
1646	        self.num_module = num_embeddings

1648	        if cat_cardinalities:

1650	# equals its cardinality.

1654	            self.cat_module = None

1656	        self.ensemble_view = EnsembleView(k=_get_required_kwarg(backbone_kwargs, 'k'))

1658	            d_in=sum(d_features),  # Flat representation size.
1659	            start_scaling_init_chunks=(
1660	                None

1662	                else d_features
1663	            ),
1664	            **backbone_kwargs,  # type: ignore[misc]
1665	        )
1666	        self.output = (
1667	            None
1668	            if d_out is None

1670	                self.backbone.get_original_output_shape()[0], d_out, k=self.backbone.k
1671	            )
1672	        )
1673	
1674	        self._n_num_features = n_num_features
1675	        self._n_cat_features = len(cat_cardinalities)
1676	
1677	    @classmethod
1678	    def make(cls, **kwargs) -> 'TabM':
1679	        """Create TabM.
1680	
1681	        Compared to `TabM.__init__`, this function does not require setting all
1682	        model-related arguments (data-related arguments are still required).
1683	        More precisely:
1684	
1685	        * The missing model arguments will be set to their default values.
1686	        * The default model argument values are *not* constant, i.e. they depend on the
1687	          provided arguments.
1688	        * Explicitly provided model arguments take precedence over their default values.
1689	
1690	        Args:
1691	            kwargs: the arguments for `TabM.__init__`.
1692	        """
1693	        has_num_embeddings = kwargs.get('num_embeddings') is not None
1694	        default_arch_type: TabMArchitectureType = 'tabm'
1695	        defaults = {
1696	            'n_blocks': 2 if has_num_embeddings else 3,
1697	            'd_block': 512,
1698	            'dropout': 0.1,
1699	            'activation': 'ReLU',
1700	            'k': 32,
1701	            'arch_type': default_arch_type,
1702	            'start_scaling_init': (
1703	                None
1704	                if kwargs.get('arch_type', default_arch_type) == 'tabm-packed'
1705	                else 'normal'
1706	                if has_num_embeddings
1707	                else 'random-signs'
1708	            ),
1709	        }
1710	        return TabM(**(defaults | kwargs))
1711	
1712	    @property
1713	    def k(self) -> int:
1714	        """The number of submodels."""
1715	        return self.backbone.k
1716	
1717	    @staticmethod
1718	    def _get_batch_info(
1719	        x_num: Optional[Tensor], x_cat: Optional[Tensor]
1720	    ) -> tuple[int, int]:
1721	        if x_num is not None:
1722	            batch_size = len(x_num)
1723	            ndim = x_num.ndim
1724	            if x_cat is not None and (x_cat.ndim != ndim or len(x_cat) != batch_size):
1725	                raise ValueError(
1726	                    f'Incompatible shapes of x_num and x_cat:'
1727	                    f' {len(x_num)=} and {len(x_cat)=}'
1728	                )
1729	            return batch_size, ndim
1730	        elif x_cat is not None:
1731	            return len(x_cat), x_cat.ndim
1732	        else:
1733	            raise ValueError('Both x_num and x_cat are None')
1734	
1735	    def _check_input(
1736	        self, x_name: str, argname: str, n_features: int, *, x: Optional[Tensor]
1737	    ) -> None:
1738	        if n_features == 0:
1739	            if x is not None:
1740	                raise RuntimeError(
1741	                    f'Based on {argname} passed to the constructor,'
1742	                    f' {x_name} must be None'
1743	                )
1744	        else:
1745	            if x is None:
1746	                raise RuntimeError(
1747	                    f'Based on {argname} passed to the constructor,'
1748	                    f' {x_name} must not be None'
1749	                )
1750	            if x.ndim != 2 and x.ndim != 3:
1751	                raise ValueError(
1752	                    'The input must have either two or three dimensions, however:'
1753	                    f' {x_name}.ndim={x.ndim}'
1754	                )
1755	            if x.shape[-1] != n_features:
1756	                raise RuntimeError(
1757	                    f'Based on {argname} passed to the constructor,'
1758	                    f'the input {x_name} must be a tensor of the shape'
1759	                    f' (batch_size, {n_features}), however: {x_name}.shape={x.shape}'
1760	                )
1761	
1762	    def _reshape_input_to_2d(self, x: Optional[Tensor]) -> Optional[Tensor]:
1763	        if x is None:
1764	            return x
1765	        elif x.ndim == 3:
1766	            # (B, K, D) -> (B * K, D)
1767	            return x.flatten(0, -2)
1768	        else:
1769	            assert x.ndim == 2, _INTERNAL_ERROR_MESSAGE
1770	            return x
1771	
1772	    def forward(
1773	        self, x_num: Optional[Tensor] = None, x_cat: Optional[Tensor] = None
1774	    ) -> Tensor:
1775	        """Do the forward pass.
1776	
1777	        Args:
1778	            x_num: the numerical features. The data type must be float.
1779	            x_cat: the categorical features. The data type must be long.
1780	                The i-th feature must take values in ``range(0, cat_cardinalities[i])``,
1781	                where ``cat_cardinalities`` is the list passed to the constructor.
1782	        """
1783	        self._check_input('x_num', 'n_num_features', self._n_num_features, x=x_num)
1784	        self._check_input('x_cat', 'cat_cardinalities', self._n_cat_features, x=x_cat)
1785	
1786	        batch_size, ndim = self._get_batch_info(x_num, x_cat)
1787	        x_num = self._reshape_input_to_2d(x_num)
1788	        x_cat = self._reshape_input_to_2d(x_cat)
1789	
1790	        x_: list[Tensor] = []
1791	        if x_num is not None:
1792	            x_.append(x_num if self.num_module is None else self.num_module(x_num))

1794	            assert self.cat_module is not None, _INTERNAL_ERROR_MESSAGE

1797	
1798	        if ndim == 3:
1799	            # Unflatten the first dimension back to the original shape.
1800	            x = x.unflatten(0, (batch_size, self.backbone.k))  # (B * K, D) -> (B, K, D)
1801	
1802	        x = self.ensemble_view(x)
1803	        x = self.backbone(x)
1804	        if self.output is not None:
1805	            x = self.output(x)

1	"""A simple test runner for Python code blocks in Markdown files.
2	
3	This script does not follow any formal Markdown format: it simply reads the input file
4	line by line, and lines starting with "<!-- test " are treated as test declarations.
5	For details, see the source code.
6	"""
7	
8	import argparse
9	import itertools
10	import sys
11	from pathlib import Path
12	from typing import Any
13	
14	import tomli
15	
16	
17	def main(argv: list[str]) -> None:
18	    assert Path(__file__).absolute() in Path.cwd().iterdir()
19	
20	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
21	        description='Test Python code blocks in a Markdown file.'
22	    )
23	    parser.add_argument('path', metavar='PATH', help='The markdown file.')
24	    args = parser.parse_args(argv[1:])
25	
26	    shared_globals: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
27	    try:
28	        exec(
29	            tomli.loads(Path('pyproject.toml').read_text())
30	            .get('tool', {})
31	            .get('test_code_blocks', {})
32	            .get('setup', 'pass'),
33	            {},
34	            shared_globals,
35	        )
36	    except Exception as err:
37	        raise RuntimeError(
38	            'Failed to execute the test setup from pyproject.toml'
39	        ) from err
40	    named_globals: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
41	
42	    lines = enumerate(Path(args.path).read_text().splitlines())
43	    n_tests = 0
44	    for index, line in lines:
45	        # >>> If the line is not a start of test, go to the next one.
46	        if not line.startswith('<!-- test '):
47	            continue
48	
49	        test_header = line
50	        if not test_header.endswith(' -->'):
51	            raise RuntimeError(f'Invalid test header: {test_header}')
52	        if not next(lines)[1].startswith('```python'):
53	            raise RuntimeError(
54	                f'The test header "{test_header}"'
55	                ' is not followed by a Python code blocks'
56	            )
57	
58	        # >>> Assemble and print the code block.
59	        print('-' * 80)
60	        print('\033[4m' + f'# Test {n_tests + 1} (line {index})' + '\033[0m')
61	        print()
62	        testsource = '\n'.join(
63	            itertools.takewhile(
64	                lambda line_: line_ != '```',  # Take until the end of the code block.
65	                map(lambda index_line: index_line[1], lines),  # Drop line indices.
66	            ),
67	        )
68	        print(testsource)
69	        print()
70	
71	        # >>> Parse the test header and prepare globals.
72	        # (A) <!-- test -->
73	        #     A simple test that relies only on the global setup from pyproject.toml.
74	        # (B) <!-- test name -->
75	        #     A named test that preserves its globals between code blocks.
76	        # (C) <!-- test name new_name -->
77	        #     A fork of one named test (name) into a new named test (new_name).
78	        #     The globals of the original test (name) remain unchanged.
79	
80	        # (A)
81	        if test_header == '<!-- test -->':
82	            test_globals = shared_globals.copy()
83	
84	        else:
85	            # ['<!--', 'test', name[, new_name], '-->']
86	            test_header_parts = test_header.strip().split()
87	
88	            # (B)
89	            if len(test_header_parts) == 4:
90	                name = test_header_parts[2]
91	                test_globals = named_globals.get(name)
92	                if test_globals is None:
93	                    test_globals = shared_globals.copy()
94	                    named_globals[name] = test_globals
95	
96	            # (C)
97	            elif len(test_header_parts) == 5:
98	                name = test_header_parts[2]
99	                new_name = test_header_parts[3]
100	                # '_' is a special value of new_name:
101	                # The header "<!-- test A _ -->" means that the test A is forked
102	                # (i.e. its globals are protected from changes), but the newly created
103	                # globals are not stored anywhere.
104	
105	                if new_name != '_' and new_name in named_globals:
106	                    raise RuntimeError(
107	                        f'Invalid test header: {test_header}'
108	                        f' (test "{new_name}" already exists)'
109	                    )
110	                if name == new_name:
111	                    raise RuntimeError(
112	                        f'Invalid test header: {test_header}'
113	                        ' (test names must be different)'
114	                    )
115	                if name not in named_globals:
116	                    raise RuntimeError(
117	                        f'Invalid test header: {test_header} (the test "{name}" does'
118	                        ' not have any state yet, so it cannot be forked)'
119	                    )
120	
121	                test_globals = shared_globals.copy()
122	                test_globals.update(named_globals[name])
123	                if new_name != '_':
124	                    named_globals[new_name] = test_globals
125	
126	            else:
127	                raise RuntimeError(f'Invalid test header: {test_header}')
128	
129	        exec(testsource, test_globals)
130	        del test_globals
131	        n_tests += 1
132	
133	    print('-' * 80)
134	    print(f'\033[32m\033[1mSUCCESS ({n_tests}/{n_tests})\033[0m')
135	
136	
137	if __name__ == '__main__':
138	    main(sys.argv)

30	def init_rsqrt_uniform_(x: Tensor, d: int) -> Tensor:
31	    assert d > 0
32	    d_rsqrt = d**-0.5
33	    return nn.init.uniform_(x, -d_rsqrt, d_rsqrt)

36	@torch.inference_mode()
37	def init_random_signs_(x: Tensor) -> Tensor:
38	    return x.bernoulli_(0.5).mul_(2).add_(-1)

44	class NLinear(nn.Module):
45	    """N linear layers applied in parallel to N disjoint parts of the input.
46	
47	    **Shape**
48	
49	    - Input: ``(B, N, in_features)``
50	    - Output: ``(B, N, out_features)``
51	
52	    The i-th linear layer is applied to the i-th matrix of the shape (B, in_features).
53	
54	    Technically, this is a simplified version of delu.nn.NLinear:
55	    https://yura52.github.io/delu/stable/api/generated/delu.nn.NLinear.html.
56	    The difference is that this layer supports only 3D inputs
57	    with exactly one batch dimension. By contrast, delu.nn.NLinear supports
58	    any number of batch dimensions.
59	    """
60	
61	    def __init__(
62	        self, n: int, in_features: int, out_features: int, bias: bool = True
63	    ) -> None:
64	        super().__init__()
65	        self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.empty(n, in_features, out_features))
66	        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.empty(n, out_features)) if bias else None
67	        self.reset_parameters()
68	
69	    def reset_parameters(self):
70	        d = self.weight.shape[-2]
71	        init_rsqrt_uniform_(self.weight, d)
72	        if self.bias is not None:
73	            init_rsqrt_uniform_(self.bias, d)
74	
75	    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
76	        assert x.ndim == 3
77	        assert x.shape[-(self.weight.ndim - 1) :] == self.weight.shape[:-1]
78	
79	        x = x.transpose(0, 1)
80	        x = x @ self.weight
81	        x = x.transpose(0, 1)
82	        if self.bias is not None:
83	            x = x + self.bias
84	        return x
85

87	class OneHotEncoding0d(nn.Module):
88	    # Input:  (*, n_cat_features=len(cardinalities))
89	    # Output: (*, sum(cardinalities))
90	
91	    def __init__(self, cardinalities: list[int]) -> None:
92	        super().__init__()
93	        self._cardinalities = cardinalities
94	
95	    def forward(self, x: Tensor) -> Tensor:
96	        assert x.ndim >= 1
97	        assert x.shape[-1] == len(self._cardinalities)
98	
99	        return torch.cat(
100	            [
101	                # NOTE
102	                # This is a quick hack to support out-of-vocabulary categories.
103	                #
104	                # Recall that lib.data.transform_cat encodes categorical features
105	                # as follows:
106	                # - In-vocabulary values receive indices from `range(cardinality)`.
107	                # - All out-of-vocabulary values (i.e. new categories in validation
108	                #   and test data that are not presented in the training data)
109	                #   receive the index `cardinality`.
110	                #
111	                # As such, the line below will produce the standard one-hot encoding for
112	                # known categories, and the all-zeros encoding for unknown categories.
113	                # This may not be the best approach to deal with unknown values,
114	                # but should be enough for our purposes.
115	                nn.functional.one_hot(x[..., i], cardinality + 1)[..., :-1]
116	                for i, cardinality in enumerate(self._cardinalities)
117	            ],
118	            -1,
119	        )

150	class LinearEfficientEnsemble(nn.Module):
151	    """
152	    This layer is a more configurable version of the "BatchEnsemble" layer
153	    from the paper
154	    "BatchEnsemble: An Alternative Approach to Efficient Ensemble and Lifelong Learning"
155	    (link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2002.06715).
156	
157	    First, this layer allows to select only some of the "ensembled" parts:
158	    - the input scaling  (r_i in the BatchEnsemble paper)
159	    - the output scaling (s_i in the BatchEnsemble paper)
160	    - the output bias    (not mentioned in the BatchEnsemble paper,
161	                          but is presented in public implementations)
162	
163	    Second, the initialization of the scaling weights is configurable
164	    through the `scaling_init` argument.
165	
166	    NOTE
167	    The term "adapter" is used in the TabM paper only to tell the story.
168	    The original BatchEnsemble paper does NOT use this term. So this class also
169	    avoids the term "adapter".
170	    """
171	
172	    r: None | Tensor
173	    s: None | Tensor
174	    bias: None | Tensor
175	
176	    def __init__(
177	        self,
178	        in_features: int,
179	        out_features: int,
180	        bias: bool = True,
181	        *,
182	        k: int,
183	        ensemble_scaling_in: bool,
184	        ensemble_scaling_out: bool,
185	        ensemble_bias: bool,
186	        scaling_init: Literal['ones', 'random-signs'],
187	    ):
188	        assert k > 0
189	        if ensemble_bias:
190	            assert bias
191	        super().__init__()
192	
193	        self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.empty(out_features, in_features))
194	        self.register_parameter(
195	            'r',
196	            (
197	                nn.Parameter(torch.empty(k, in_features))
198	                if ensemble_scaling_in
199	                else None
200	            ),  # type: ignore[code]
201	        )
202	        self.register_parameter(
203	            's',
204	            (
205	                nn.Parameter(torch.empty(k, out_features))
206	                if ensemble_scaling_out
207	                else None
208	            ),  # type: ignore[code]
209	        )
210	        self.register_parameter(
211	            'bias',
212	            (
213	                nn.Parameter(torch.empty(out_features))  # type: ignore[code]
214	                if bias and not ensemble_bias
215	                else nn.Parameter(torch.empty(k, out_features))
216	                if ensemble_bias
217	                else None
218	            ),
219	        )
220	
221	        self.in_features = in_features
222	        self.out_features = out_features
223	        self.k = k
224	        self.scaling_init = scaling_init
225	
226	        self.reset_parameters()
227	
228	    def reset_parameters(self):
229	        init_rsqrt_uniform_(self.weight, self.in_features)
230	        scaling_init_fn = {'ones': nn.init.ones_, 'random-signs': init_random_signs_}[
231	            self.scaling_init
232	        ]
233	        if self.r is not None:
234	            scaling_init_fn(self.r)
235	        if self.s is not None:
236	            scaling_init_fn(self.s)
237	        if self.bias is not None:
238	            bias_init = torch.empty(
239	                # NOTE: the shape of bias_init is (out_features,) not (k, out_features).
240	                # It means that all biases have the same initialization.
241	                # This is similar to having one shared bias plus
242	                # k zero-initialized non-shared biases.
243	                self.out_features,
244	                dtype=self.weight.dtype,
245	                device=self.weight.device,
246	            )
247	            bias_init = init_rsqrt_uniform_(bias_init, self.in_features)
248	            with torch.inference_mode():
249	                self.bias.copy_(bias_init)
250	
251	    def forward(self, x: Tensor) -> Tensor:
252	        # x.shape == (B, K, D)
253	        assert x.ndim == 3
254	
255	        # >>> The equation (5) from the BatchEnsemble paper (arXiv v2).
256	        if self.r is not None:
257	            x = x * self.r
258	        x = x @ self.weight.T
259	        if self.s is not None:
260	            x = x * self.s
261	        # <<<
262	
263	        if self.bias is not None:
264	            x = x + self.bias
265	        return x

268	class MLP(nn.Module):
269	    def __init__(
270	        self,
271	        *,
272	        d_in: None | int = None,
273	        d_out: None | int = None,
274	        n_blocks: int,
275	        d_block: int,
276	        dropout: float,
277	        activation: str = 'ReLU',
278	    ) -> None:
279	        super().__init__()
280	
281	        d_first = d_block if d_in is None else d_in
282	        self.blocks = nn.ModuleList(
283	            [
284	                nn.Sequential(
285	                    nn.Linear(d_first if i == 0 else d_block, d_block),
286	                    getattr(nn, activation)(),
287	                    nn.Dropout(dropout),
288	                )
289	                for i in range(n_blocks)
290	            ]
291	        )
292	        self.output = None if d_out is None else nn.Linear(d_block, d_out)
293	
294	    def forward(self, x: Tensor) -> Tensor:
295	        for block in self.blocks:
296	            x = block(x)
297	        if self.output is not None:
298	            x = self.output(x)
299	        return x

302	def make_efficient_ensemble(module: nn.Module, EnsembleLayer, **kwargs) -> None:
303	    """Replace linear layers with efficient ensembles of linear layers.
304	
305	    NOTE
306	    In the paper, there are no experiments with networks with normalization layers.
307	    Perhaps, their trainable weights (the affine transformations) also need
308	    "ensemblification" as in the paper about "FiLM-Ensemble".
309	    Additional experiments are required to make conclusions.
310	    """
311	    for name, submodule in list(module.named_children()):
312	        if isinstance(submodule, nn.Linear):
313	            module.add_module(
314	                name,
315	                EnsembleLayer(
316	                    in_features=submodule.in_features,
317	                    out_features=submodule.out_features,
318	                    bias=submodule.bias is not None,
319	                    **kwargs,
320	                ),
321	            )
322	        else:
323	            make_efficient_ensemble(submodule, EnsembleLayer, **kwargs)

326	def _get_first_ensemble_layer(backbone: MLP) -> LinearEfficientEnsemble:
327	    if isinstance(backbone, MLP):

333	@torch.inference_mode()
334	def _init_first_adapter(
335	    weight: Tensor,
336	    distribution: Literal['normal', 'random-signs'],
337	    init_sections: list[int],
338	) -> None:
339	    """Initialize the first adapter.
340	
341	    NOTE
342	    The `init_sections` argument is a historical artifact that accidentally leaked
343	    from irrelevant experiments to the final models. Perhaps, the code related
344	    to `init_sections` can be simply removed, but this was not tested.
345	    """
346	    assert weight.ndim == 2
347	    assert weight.shape[1] == sum(init_sections)
348	
349	    if distribution == 'normal':
350	        init_fn_ = nn.init.normal_
351	    elif distribution == 'random-signs':
352	        init_fn_ = init_random_signs_
353	    else:
354	        raise ValueError(f'Unknown distribution: {distribution}')
355	
356	    section_bounds = [0, *torch.tensor(init_sections).cumsum(0).tolist()]
357	    for i in range(len(init_sections)):
358	        # NOTE
359	        # As noted above, this section-based initialization is an arbitrary historical
360	        # artifact. Consider the first adapter of one ensemble member.
361	        # This adapter vector is implicitly split into "sections",
362	        # where one section corresponds to one feature. The code below ensures that
363	        # the adapter weights in one section are initialized with the same random value
364	        # from the given distribution.
365	        w = torch.empty((len(weight), 1), dtype=weight.dtype, device=weight.device)
366	        init_fn_(w)
367	        weight[:, section_bounds[i] : section_bounds[i + 1]] = w

393	def default_zero_weight_decay_condition(
394	    module_name: str, module: nn.Module, parameter_name: str, parameter: nn.Parameter
395	):
396	    from rtdl_num_embeddings import _Periodic

398	    del module_name, parameter

410	def make_parameter_groups(
411	    module: nn.Module,
412	    zero_weight_decay_condition=default_zero_weight_decay_condition,
413	    custom_groups: None | list[dict[str, Any]] = None,
414	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
415	    if custom_groups is None:
416	        custom_groups = []
417	    custom_params = frozenset(
418	        itertools.chain.from_iterable(group['params'] for group in custom_groups)
419	    )
420	    assert len(custom_params) == sum(
421	        len(group['params']) for group in custom_groups
422	    ), 'Parameters in custom_groups must not intersect'
423	    zero_wd_params = frozenset(

425	        for mn, m in module.named_modules()
426	        for pn, p in m.named_parameters()

428	    )

437	        default_group,

447	    """MLP & TabM."""
448	
449	    def __init__(
450	        self,

22	def main(
23	    path: str | Path,
24	    n_seeds: int = DEFAULT_N_SEEDS,
25	    function: None | str = None,
26	    *,
27	    force: bool = False,

31

39	        evaluation_dir = path.with_name(path.name.replace('tuning', 'evaluation'))
40	        evaluation_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
41	
42	    elif path.name.endswith('-evaluation'):
43	        assert function is not None
44	        from_tuning = False
45	        function_qualname = function
46	        evaluation_dir = path
47	        template_config = lib.load_config(evaluation_dir / '0')
48	
49	    else:
50	        raise ValueError(f'Bad input path: {path}')
51	    del path
52	
53	    function_ = lib.import_(function_qualname)
54	    for seed in range(n_seeds):
55	        output = evaluation_dir / str(seed)
56	        config_path = output.with_suffix('.toml')
57	        done = (output / 'DONE').exists()
58	        if config_path.exists() and not done:
59	            if output.exists():
60	                logger.warning(f'Removing the incomplete output {output}')
61	                shutil.rmtree(output)
62	            if from_tuning or seed > 0:
63	                config_path.unlink()
64	
65	        config = deepcopy(template_config)
66	        config['seed'] = seed
67	        if 'catboost' in function_qualname:
68	            if config['model']['task_type'] == 'GPU':
69	                config['model']['task_type'] = 'CPU'  # this is crucial for good results
70	                thread_count = config['model'].get('thread_count', 1)

72	
73	        try:

75	                lib.dump_config(output, config)
76	            function_(config, output, force=force)
77	        except Exception:
78	            if from_tuning or seed > 0:
79	                config_path.unlink(True)
80	            shutil.rmtree(output, True)
81	            raise
